notes: Strong overall implementation. SECURITY (7): timingSafeEqual for HMAC is correct, rate limiting and body size limits present, good security headers. However, the HMAC algorithm is parsed directly from the attacker-controlled signature header (line 210) with no allowlist — an attacker can force weaker algorithms like md5. X-Forwarded-For trusted without proxy validation enables rate-limit bypass via IP spoofing. ALLOWED_ORIGIN defaults to '*'. ERROR_HANDLING (8): Well-structured custom error hierarchy, global handler checks headersSent, uncaughtException/unhandledRejection trigger graceful shutdown, retry failures logged with full context, structured error responses with requestId. Minor: PayloadTooLargeError in stream could race with subsequent data events. BEST_PRACTICES (8): Clean separation of concerns (BoundedTTLMap, RateLimiter, RetryExecutor), environment validation with fail-fast, configurable via env vars with sensible defaults, structured JSON logging, requestId tracing, Content-Type validation, Buffer.byteLength for Content-Length, cleanupInterval.unref(), graceful shutdown with connection draining. Minor inconsistency: console.error at startup vs logger elsewhere. No tests or JSDoc. ROBUSTNESS (6): Bounded dedup map with TTL and eviction prevents memory growth, rate limiter cleanup prevents leak, idempotency with optimistic locking. However: in-memory only — all dedup state lost on restart (no persistence layer). No handler timeout — a stuck handler blocks indefinitely inside retry loop. No dead-letter queue or alerting for permanently failed events. Minor TOCTOU between has() and get() on dedup map (lines 446-447). Failed events marked but never retried or surfaced for manual review.
